title: Quick Wins: RankedSearch Cache & Lazy Loading
impact: 60-80% faster fallback searches; Near-instant startup for large graphs

tasks.description: Add TokenizedEntity Interface - Create interface with entity reference, tokens Set<string>, and document string for caching tokenization results in RankedSearch
acceptanceCriteria: TokenizedEntity interface defined with entity, tokens, and document fields
Interface used for type safety in token cache

tasks.description: Implement RankedSearch Fallback Token Cache - Add tokenCache Map<string, TokenizedEntity> and idfCache Map<string, number> to RankedSearch, implement getOrComputeTokens() method with lastModified-based invalidation
testCategories: Token cache hit for unchanged entities
Token cache miss for modified entities
IDF cache rebuild on significant graph changes
acceptanceCriteria: tokenCache: Map<string, TokenizedEntity> property added
idfCache: Map<string, number> property added
lastEntityCount: number property for IDF rebuild detection
getOrComputeTokens() checks lastModified for cache validity
IDF cache rebuilds when entity count changes >10%
Fallback search uses cached tokens instead of recomputing

tasks.description: Implement Lazy Graph Loading in SQLiteStorage - Currently loadCache() is called in initialize() at line 113. Add cacheLoaded boolean and loadingPromise, implement ensureCacheLoaded() method, modify loadGraph() to call ensureCacheLoaded() instead of immediate load, add getEntity() fast path that queries DB directly without full cache load
testCategories: Lazy load on first full graph access
Direct DB query for single entity lookup
acceptanceCriteria: cacheLoaded: boolean property added (default false)
loadingPromise: Promise<void> | null for concurrent load handling
ensureCacheLoaded() method that loads cache only once
loadGraph() calls ensureCacheLoaded() before returning cache
getEntity(name) queries DB directly if cache not loaded
Constructor/initialize() no longer calls loadCache() immediately
Startup time reduced to <5ms regardless of graph size

tasks.description: Add Lazy Loading and Cache Tests - Test lazy loading behavior, token cache effectiveness, and startup time improvements
testCategories: Token cache improves subsequent search performance
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies cache not loaded on construction
Test verifies cache loaded on first loadGraph() call
Test verifies getEntity() works without cache
Test verifies token cache hit improves performance
Startup time test with 10K entities < 50ms
10+ new tests added

successCriteria: RankedSearch token cache reduces repeated fallback search time by 60-80%
Startup time independent of graph size (~5ms)
Single entity lookups work without loading full graph
All existing tests continue to pass
New tests verify lazy loading and caching behavior
notes: This sprint completes the Quick Wins phase. The RankedSearch fallback cache addresses the performance issue when TF-IDF index files aren't available. Lazy graph loading dramatically improves startup time for large graphs by deferring the full cache load until first access. These optimizations have no API changes and are backward compatible.
